chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ مَنْ رَضِيَ بِالْيَمِينِ فَحُلِفَ لَهُ فَلَا دَعْوَى لَهُ بَعْدَ الْيَمِينِ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 15 – The one who is pleased with the oath, so it is sworn for him, so there is no claim for him after the swearing of the oath, even if there was proof for him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/286)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/286)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الْمَالُ فَيَجْحَدُهُ قَالَ إِنِ اسْتَحْلَفَهُ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ شَيْئاً وَ إِنْ تَرَكَهُ وَ لَمْ يَسْتَحْلِفْهُ فَهُوَ عَلَى حَقِّهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from AL Fazl Bin Shazaan, both together from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibrahim Bin Abdul Hameed, from Khazar Al Nakhai’e,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who happened to have wealth for him over the man, but he argued it (disputed it). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he (the defendant) were to swear, so it is not for him (the claimant) that he should take anything, and if he (the defendant) leaves it and does not swear, so he (the claimant) is upon his right’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-FIamid from Khidr al-Nakha’iy who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who has a certain right on another man who denies it, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If he has made him to take an oath, then he does not have any right; but if he has not made him to take an oath, then his right is in place.’”
